entry.title: Re: Restrict Posting of Both Cost Center & WBS Element To...
entry.content:
Current Posting Behavior:

  • Actual postings will be recorded in the WBS element, while the cost center will serve as a statistical posting (if actual posting to the cost center is required, the WBS element must be defined as statistical).
  • If this approach is not suitable, consider implementing custom validation rules using:
    • Custom Fields and Logic App (App ID: F1481)
    • Manage Substitution and Validation Rules App (App ID: F4406)

entry.title: UADE Honored at SAP Academic Community Conference LAC 2026 — Educational Excellence Membership Award
entry.content:
[This text is a copy of the translation of the original linkedin post in Spanish, which you can find here: Post | LinkedIn]

"Education does not change the world. It changes people, and people change the world." Paulo Freire

That quote appeared in one of the opening presentations at the SAP Academic Community Conference LAC 2026, in São Leopoldo, Brazil, and stayed with me throughout the three days of the event.

I had the privilege of representing UADE at SAP Labs Latin America, alongside fellow academics and academic leaders from across Latin America and the Caribbean.

It was three intense days of sessions, workshops, and conversations about how to continue integrating technology into the classroom and how to prepare future professionals with concrete tools to meet the demands of the job market.

We also came back with news that fills us with pride: UADE was honored with the SAP University Alliances Educational Excellence Membership Award 2026/2027 for the LATAM and Caribbean region. 🏆

This recognition reflects the sustained work of a great team and a clear institutional commitment to bringing SAP into the education of our students, bridging the academic experience with the real demands of the professional world.

On a personal note, I am taking home new connections, ideas for the upcoming semesters, and the certainty that these gatherings truly move the needle.

I would like to extend my special thanks to the leadership at UADE for their trust and for making my participation in this event possible. Representing the university at a regional gathering of this magnitude was an enormous responsibility and, at the same time, a deeply enriching experience.

Thank you to SAP University Alliances and the SAP Academic Board Ibero-America for the organization, to all the colleagues I shared these days with, to María Cecilia Calzada de Neumann, Isadora Royer, Eduardo Baptista de Oliveira, and to the entire SAP team for organizing such a remarkable event.

#SAPUniversityAlliances #ACCLAC2026 #UADE #ExcellenceMembership #HigherEducation #SAP

SPANISH copy

“La educación no cambia el mundo. Cambia a las personas, y las personas cambian el mundo.” Paulo Freire
Esa frase apareció en una de las primeras presentaciones del SAP Academic Community Conference LAC 2026, en São Leopoldo, Brasil, y me acompañó durante los tres días del encuentro.
Tuve el privilegio de representar a UADE en SAP Labs Latin America, junto a colegas y referentes académicos de toda Latinoamérica y el Caribe.
Fueron tres jornadas intensas de sesiones, talleres y conversaciones sobre cómo seguir integrando la tecnología en el aula y cómo preparar a los futuros profesionales con herramientas concretas para los desafíos del mercado laboral.
Además, volvimos con una noticia que nos llena de orgullo: UADE fue distinguida con el SAP University Alliances Educational Excellence Membership Award 2026/2027 para la región LATAM y el Caribe. 🏆
Este reconocimiento refleja el trabajo sostenido de un gran equipo y una apuesta institucional clara por acercar SAP a la formación de nuestros estudiantes, conectando la experiencia académica con las demandas reales del mundo profesional.
Por otro lado, me llevo nuevos vínculos, ideas para los próximos cuatrimestres y la certeza de que estos espacios realmente mueven la aguja.
Quiero agradecer especialmente a las autoridades de UADE por la confianza y por haber hecho posible mi participación en este encuentro. Representar a la universidad en un espacio regional de esta magnitud fue una enorme responsabilidad y, al mismo tiempo, una experiencia profundamente enriquecedora.
Gracias a SAP University Alliances y al SAP Academic Board Ibero-America por la organización, a todos los colegas con quienes compartí estos días, a María Cecilia Calzada de Neumann, Isadora Royer, Eduardo Baptista de Oliveira y a todo el equipo de SAP por la organización de  este  destacado evento.
#SAPUniversityAlliances #ACCLAC2026 #UADE #ExcellenceMembership #EducaciónSuperior #SAP

for me the solution of @ITB_Tom hasn't worked out if I understood your solution right. The column 'Doc. title' is still empty in a SAP HANA 2023 SP3 (02/2025) FPS.
My solution is: Create the document with mail body at first and set afterwards the subject for both, the document AND the send request.

    
...
* Create document for e-mail content first without an subject (= SPACE)
DATA(lo_document_ref) = cl_document_bcs=>create_document(
                                        i_type    = 'TXT'
                                        i_text    = lt_content_txt
                                        i_subject = space ).    "!!!
lo_document_ref->set_subject( CONV #( iv_subject ) ).
lo_send_request_ref->set_message_subject( CONV #( lv_subject ) ).
...

entry.title: Custom report : Stock In‑Out & Balance Report with Transaction currency
entry.content:
Hello experts, 

(Product: S/4HANA Public Cloud)

Our client use Company Code Currency A, but daily transactions are settled in Currency B or C.
 
Inventory reports exported from SAP show inventory values calculated by moving average price under Company Code Currency A.
 
The client requires all inventory reports to be displayed based on transaction currency B or C. Purchasing, sales and inventory data should all be shown in transaction currency B or C.
 
We plan to implemente via Custom CDS View + Custom Query, we can obtain Material Documents items of all movement types, their posting dates, materials and quantities from view: 
I_MaterialDocumentItem_2.
 
For GR material document with movement type 101, we can get the Document Currency(transaction currency) and Net Price Amount from view: I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01 by linking PO and PO Item within the material document item.
 
The current challenge is to calculate the material moving average price in transaction currency without exchange rate conversion.
 
Since 'Out Amount', 'opening inventory amount' and 'closing inventory amount' all need to be calculated using the moving average price under transaction currency, how to implement step‑by‑step rolling calculation of moving average price?
 
and how to get the Opening Inventory Qty ? 
 

thanks a lot.

Best regards.

entry.title: SF to ECC Replication - Employee Org Assignment Data in Pending Status
entry.content:
Hello! Has anyone noticed entries in Data Replication Monitor for Replication Content Type: Employee Org. Assignment staying in "Pending" status forever? No Messages are generated for these entries and a manual workaround is to execute t.codes 1.SFIOM_VIEW_ORG_REQS & 2. SFIOM_VIEW_REQUESTS that pushes the data from staging area to the ECC database tables. After that the entry in DRM updates to successful status. I have not noticed any issues with data quality. We are core hybrid and did not have any changes to config or development in SFSF, CPI or ECC recently. 

I am looking for the root cause and a permanent solution to this issue.

entry.title: Re: cost element move to other existing ccs OKTZ does not...
entry.content:
ok so. I did a clean test in the sandbox  - produced existing project, posted amounts in CC B accounts , closed the period - the result landed in CC B. 

I looked again in production - out of 20 SFG products 19 post to CC A, and one to CC B. So the system works but something went wrong.  Partially wrong. 

Eventually I raised OSS and the team reset the CC split for current and next period. Now all are in CC B.

The most important lesson learnt here - you can change GL accounts between cost components without special precedure. 

thanks all. case closed.

entry.title: Re: From Legacy to Modern Auth: Enabling OpenID Connect in SAP BusinessObjects Step-by-step
entry.content:
Hi @YvanB,

It is possible.

First thing, In general, if your user base is coming from SAP / Win AD / LDAP and you sync them using the respective authentication plugin and sync users... then those users will usually have respective user alias related to those authentication plugins only, and will not have "Enterprise alias", unless you manually create Enterprise alias for such users. If this is the case, then by design, those users will not be able to login using Enterprise authentication, since they will not have the alias itself, they will be able to login using the respective authentication plugins.


But, for some reason,
you might have created Enterprise aliases for the user accounts synced form the SAP / LDAP / Windows AD.... 

Or
you might have created the users first with Enterprise Authentication and later you might have synced users from SAP / LDAP / Windows AD by selecting the option to just add alias if the user object exists already with the same username.

Then only... you will have the user accounts with multiple authentications enabled will exists in the system....

If this is the case... then you can either simply disable the "Enterprise alias" by unchecking the checkbox under Enable column of the Enterprise alias... Or entirely delete "Enterprise Alias" by clicking on "Delete Alias" corresponding to that alias.

You can do this for all the users in one shot by adding all the users, for whom, you do not want to allow Enterprise login to a group.... and then right click on the group and select "Account Manager" option from the right click menu... and then select the checkbox corresponding to "Enable / Disable User Aliases" section... and under that select the checkbox corresponding to "Enterprise" and then leave black the checkbox for the same row under "Enabled:" column... like shown below and then click on "Save & Close".

In this way, you can disable the "Enterprise alias" for all the users directly part of this group.... so there by those users will not be allowed to perform Enterprise login.


Hope this clarifies.
-Venkat
SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform #SAP #BOBJ

entry.title: gCTS: parallel development on the same server
entry.content:
I am currently using SAP S/4HANA with gCTS on a single DEV server, and I am trying to understand whether parallel development on the same object is possible when using different Git branches.

My scenario is the following:

  • Two developers are working in the same DEV system.

  • Each developer is using a different Git branch in gCTS.

  • Both developers need to modify the same ABAP object (for example, the same class, program, or CDS view) for different features.

Since gCTS is Git-based and supports branching, I expected that each developer could work independently in their own branch without locking the object for the other developer. However, in practice, the SAP object locking/edit mechanism still seems to prevent parallel editing on the same system.

So my question is:

Is true parallel development of the same object possible with gCTS when both developers are working in the same SAP DEV server but on different Git branches?

Or does gCTS branching only work properly for parallel development if each branch is connected to a separate development system/environment?

If anyone has experience with this scenario or knows the recommended approach for enabling parallel development of the same object with gCTS, I would really appreciate the clarification.

entry.title: Re: How to Implement SAP Business One projects successfully
entry.content:
Solid breakdown of the implementation steps. Having run 20+ SMB go-lives across Hyderabad pharma and manufacturing, I'd add one layer that's increasingly make-or-break in 2024–25: data readiness and real-time visibility are the go-live risks that cost the most money — and they're almost never on the standard checklist.

Here's the Week 3 go-live checklist I use with every client, structured around the two failure modes I see repeatedly:

Phase 1: Data Freeze & Validation (48–72 hours pre-go-live)

  • Master data lock — no changes to item master, vendor master, or chart of accounts after T-48hrs
  • Parallel inventory count: run physical count in legacy system AND SAP B1 simultaneously. Any variance >2% = go-live hold
  • Open transaction reconciliation: every open PO, SO, and AP/AR item must match between systems
  • Unit of measure conversion audit — the single most common source of post-go-live inventory report failures
  • Batch management configuration confirmed for all batch-managed materials (raw, semi-finished, finished goods)

Phase 2: Go-Live Day Readiness

  • Rollback decision criteria documented: who decides, under what conditions, what's the cutover back to legacy
  • Power users confirmed on-site (2–3 per department, trained to process every transaction type independently)
  • Go-live support team: internal champion + consultant available for 72 hours minimum
  • First transaction test: process one complete cycle (PO → GR → Invoice → Payment) in production before declaring live
  • Finance month-end check: confirm SAP B1 can close the current period within 5 business days of go-live

Phase 3: Real-Time Visibility Check (often skipped, always regretted)

  • KPI dashboards live: inventory turnover, stock-out risk, open AP/AR aging — confirm these are pulling real production data, not demo data
  • Batch cost visibility: for pharma clients especially, confirm that batch-level cost analysis is running correctly. This is where most Hyderabad pharma shops discover gaps — batch costs are visible in SAP B1, but the gap analysis between planned vs. actual batch costs requires either custom queries or a connected AI layer
  • Approval workflows tested under production load — discount approvals, purchase order thresholds, production order releases

One thing I've added to every go-live checklist in the last 18 months: a real-time operations readiness check on Day 1. Not just "can users log in" — but "can the MD or CFO see live cashflow, live inventory position, and any batch cost exceptions from a single screen without running a manual report?" If the answer is no, you haven't finished the implementation — you've just migrated data.

For Hyderabad pharma shops targeting USFDA or WHO-GMP compliance, the batch cost gap analysis piece is particularly critical. Regulatory auditors increasingly expect real-time batch genealogy and cost traceability — not a spreadsheet compiled after the fact.

Teams that use a structured go-live checklist like this average 3–4 days of post-go-live disruption. Teams that skip it average 3–4 weeks. The math is not subtle.

Happy to share the full checklist template or go deeper on any specific module. What's the implementation phase you're currently in?

— Venkata Siva Reddy Polu, TEKROI
SAP B1 implementation for Hyderabad pharma and manufacturing SMBs | TekAI: real-time operations intelligence on SAP B1

entry.title: Start Small. Think at Scale. Business Professionals Can Use SAP & Claude to Build a Business Case.
entry.content:
Note: This blog provides an actionable step-by-step proof of concept (POC) for the SAP and Anthropic partnership that a business professional can build today using SAP's free Business Accelerator Hub sandbox data and Claude. The Master Prompt is included below. A sample output is provided in the Addendum. Already familiar with this context? Skip directly to The Proof of Concept section.

Build the POC. Create the Business Case. Start the Conversation.

The recent SAP and Anthropic partnership announcement ignited a new wave of opportunities. It got me thinking about how the combined capabilities of Claude and SAP will strengthen enterprises around the globe.


At the same time, I found myself wondering. How can Business Professionals add real value to what needs to happen to put these capabilities in place for maximum success?

It reminded me of a simple metaphor. Imagine digging a new swimming pool with a shovel. You are making progress. Slowly. Painfully. Then someone pulls up with a bulldozer. The hole that would have taken weeks gets done in a day. That is the uplift the SAP and Anthropic partnership represents for organizations who are ready to act.

So how can Business Professionals help jumpstart the process?

Consider the impact of creating a simple proof of concept using SAP's own sandbox data and Claude, grounded in business case best practices, to help prioritize initiatives that can create compelling value.

The Market Opportunity

The scale of this partnership is significant. SAP and Anthropic are not simply connecting two products. They are building an Autonomous Enterprise vision where Claude powered agents operate through SAP Business AI Platform, coordinate across SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, and other SAP products via Model Context Protocol (MCP), and are accessible to users through Joule.

IDC projects AI spending driven by agentic AI will reach $1.3 trillion by 2029, growing at 31.9% year over year. In a best case scenario Gartner projects a $450 billion enterprise AI opportunity by 2035. Yet according to the DSAG Investment Report published in February 2026, among SAP customers already using AI, only 3% are running SAP Business AI in production today. The opportunity is enormous. But before Business Professionals can seize it they must consider creating POCs and business cases that will move their organization from insight to action.

The New API Policy. A Quality Framework.

It is worth highlighting a recent SAP announcement that is directly relevant to this partnership. SAP's API policy governs how autonomous external agents make sequential API calls into SAP systems.

Consider this approach as a quality framework.

Think Lean Six Sigma, ITIL, or ISO 9001 for mission critical Cloud ERP processes. Controlled variation. Trusted inputs. Governed outputs.

Claude operating inside SAP's approved architecture brings these same quality principles to agentic AI. As a Six Sigma certified practitioner I can say with confidence that controlled variation and governed outputs are not optional in mission critical processes. They are the standard.

Errors in enterprise data cannot be fully eliminated but they must be managed, minimized, and controlled continuously. They stem from uncontrolled inputs, ungoverned processes, and systems operating outside defined boundaries.

SAP's framework controls that variation at the source, reducing risk to the lowest achievable level and keeping mission critical processes operating with the precision enterprises depend on.

From Insight to Action. Building Your Case

Consider this scenario. You are a Business Professional who has just learned about the SAP and Anthropic partnership. You see the opportunity clearly. You know your organization has processes that AI can benefit, especially with Claude and SAP working together.

But where do you start?

You are not a developer. You do not have a project budget approved. You cannot stand up a Joule environment this afternoon.

What you do have is business knowledge, SAP familiarity, and a growing confidence in what Claude can do. Most importantly you have a clear sense of where the pain and inefficiencies are in your organization today.

That is enough to get started.

The path forward has two parallel tracks that reinforce each other:

  1. Delivering a proof of concept (POC) that shows what is possible
  2. Creating a business case that justifies the investment to make it real at scale.

The good news is that SAP has already given you the tools to do both.

The SAP Business Accelerator Hub was built explicitly to accelerate customer time to value. Free to access. No SAP license required. No IT ticket needed. Just a free SAP ID and a clear business question you want Claude to help answer.

The proof of concept you build today becomes the evidence that funds the enterprise deployment tomorrow. The business case you build around it gives your organization's executive sponsors the confidence to act.

This approach is how Business Professionals provide meaningful leadership, collaborating with IT counterparts and key stakeholders to move from insight to action.

The Proof of Concept  

This POC is intentionally simple and uses SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition procurement data available through the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox. It is a minimum viable product (MVP) approach designed to show what is possible without requiring engineering support, IT involvement, or a live SAP system. Every step is something a Business Professional can execute independently.

What you are about to build is a working example of agentic AI. Claude is not simply answering a question. It is reasoning over real SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition data, identifying risk, prioritizing action, and recommending next steps. That is agentic AI in action. And it is a preview of what runs inside Joule at enterprise scale.

The following steps walk you through the POC from start to finish.

What You Do

  1. Register for a free SAP ID at api.sap.com. Takes a few minutes.
  2. Go to the Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001) in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This is the current OData V4 Purchase Order API for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and one example of the many sandbox APIs available across SAP's core product portfolio.
  3. Click “Try Out” at the top. Select “Sandbox” from the dropdown on the right side. Navigate to the Purchase Order section in the left hand navigation. Click the /PurchaseOrder “GET” option. Click “Run.”
  4. Copy the JSON response that appears under “Response Body.”
  5. Open Claude at claude.ai. Paste the data and enter the Master Prompt below. Note: A free Claude account is sufficient for this POC. Claude Pro provides higher usage limits if needed.

What Claude Does

  1. Reads and interprets the SAP S/4HANA Cloud purchase order data.
  2. Identifies which exceptions represent the highest business risk.
  3. Assigns a risk score to each record with a clear rationale.
  4. Recommends specific actions for each prioritized item.
  5. Produces a scored table ready for your business case conversation.
  6. Offers to draft a supplier communication for the highest risk item.

Master Prompt

Copy and paste the following prompt into Claude along with your JSON data (as noted above). You are reviewing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition purchase order data retrieved directly from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox via the Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001).

[START PROMPT]

Review the records provided and analyze the following fields: PurchaseOrder number, Supplier, CreationDate, PurchaseOrderDate, PurchasingProcessingStatus, PurchaseOrderDeletionCode, ReleaseIsNotCompleted, and PurchasingReleaseStrategy.

Based on your analysis:

  1. Identify purchase orders that require attention. Focus on deletion flagged records, incomplete releases, unusual processing statuses, and supplier concentration patterns.
  2. Assign a priority level of High, Medium, or Low to each flagged record with a brief rationale.
  3. Recommend a specific action for each flagged item.
  4. Produce a prioritized table with the following columns: PO Number, Supplier, Creation Date, Processing Status, Deletion Flag, Release Complete, Priority Level, Recommended Action.
  5. Provide a two to three sentence plain English summary of your overall findings before the table.
  6. After the table offer to draft a supplier communication for the highest priority item.
  7. Keep your total response concise. Summary, table, and offer only. No additional commentary unless asked.

[END PROMPT]

What You Just Built

A working example of agentic AI reasoning over real SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition procurement data. Built by a Business Professional. Using SAP's own tools. View the sample output in the Addendum below

This is your building block. The cornerstone of your business case. And the start of a meaningful conversation with your IT counterparts, key stakeholders, and executive sponsors about what is possible and the value this use case can deliver.

Start small. Think at scale.

From POC to Production

This POC uses the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox as a starting point outside of SAP's production architecture. That is intentional. The goal is to demonstrate the concept quickly and accessibly. Free sandbox data. No infrastructure required.

Joule Studio is where developers and business teams can go next. A governed, enterprise grade environment where Business Professionals and developers collaborate to build both pre-built and custom agents tailored to their specific processes, data, and workflows. Joule Studio requires SAP BTP, a Joule subscription, SAP Build licensing, and IT provisioning to activate. It is the right next step. See Activate Joule Studio in 5 Simple Steps for full prerequisites.  

Complementary approaches. Each with its own role.

In production this same capability runs natively inside SAP Business AI Platform through the MCP Gateway. It is accessible to users through Joule. Everything you just demonstrated is the reasoning capability that sits behind the potential production implementation. The logic transfers directly.

In this POC the Hub provides everything you need to show what is possible.

Building the Business Case

A strong business case requires the right elements addressed with clarity and evidence. The POC you just built directly addresses two of the most critical. It defines the problem in concrete terms and demonstrates measurable value, ROI potential, and a clear view of what is possible.

The implementation path and risk and governance elements are addressed by SAP's approved architecture. The path runs through SAP Business AI Platform, Joule, and the production framework SAP and Anthropic have built together.

That represents a credible and governed foundation for any business case conversation.


Security and Data You Can Depend On

The same security and governance framework that protects your SAP environment today protects every action Claude takes inside it. The controls do not change because AI is involved. They extend to cover it.

Every action is governed by the same role-based access controls your organization operates under today. Every decision Claude makes is traceable through a full audit trail.

Connecting external data sources to the SAP environment requires proper integration through the SAP Integration Suite, ensuring data flows through governed, secure pathways before Claude ever touches it.

The Path Forward

The SAP and Anthropic partnership is designed for enterprise scale, grounded in trust and quality outcomes. Today, Business Professionals can create a tangible starting point and play a leadership role in the conversation with IT and business stakeholders about initiatives that can create lasting value.

SAP and Anthropic have announced plans to bring Claude to SAP Business AI Platform. Joule Studio, the development environment where agents are built and deployed, has general availability planned for later this year.

Now is the time to build your POC and business case so your organization is ready.

Take it one step at a time.

Start small. Think at scale.

Join the Conversation

Your likes and comments are welcome and appreciated.

  • Are you excited about the SAP and Anthropic partnership?
  • Were you aware of the SAP Business Accelerator Hub?
  • What are you working on with Claude or your favorite Assistant AI that can be applied to the Autonomous Enterprise vision?

About the Author

Rich Blumberg is an award-winning writer and author of two Amazon published books, Novi’s Journey to the Magic Garden and Job Seeking Warriors – A Mentors Guide to Winning. He's the President of World Sales Solutions, LLC, (WSS) a management consulting, low-code, no-code, and AI services firm. For more than 20 years he has worked as an SAP consultant partnering with SAP and their ecosystem organizations around the globe. Outside of work Rich serves as a Drexel University Alumni Board of Governors Emeritus, an ENGin English tutor supporting Ukrainian citizens, and an aspiring guitar player.

Related Blogs

  • How the AI Content Lifecycle Turns Complexity into Clarity at Scale(Part 2)
  • AI-Powered Knowledge Gap Analysis and Content Lifecycle Governance(Part 1)
  • You Can Build an Agentic AI Workflow. No Coding, No Engineering, No Problem.
  • AI at Warp Speed: Lessons from the Horse and Buggy Collapse

Addendum SAP S/4HANA Cloud POC Output

Purchase Order Analysis using the SAP Business Accelerator Hub Sandbox and Claude

Summary

Of the 50 purchase orders reviewed, 6 require immediate attention. Two records carry both a deletion flag and an incomplete release status, representing the highest risk. Four additional records show a deletion code with a completed processing status, suggesting they were cancelled but may warrant confirmation. Supplier 17300001 dominates the dataset with over 60% concentration, which itself warrants a supplier risk review.

Note: In a production environment this same analysis can run across thousands of purchase orders in seconds.

Prioritized Purchase Order Exception Table

Draft Supplier Communication

Highest Priority Item: Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022 | Supplier USSU-VSF01

Subject: Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022 – Urgent Status Clarification Required

Dear USSU-VSF01 Team,

I am writing regarding two purchase orders currently showing an unresolved status in our SAP S/4HANA system that require your immediate attention.

Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022, both dated August 18, 2016, are currently flagged for deletion while the approval release process remains incomplete. This combination of statuses prevents us from properly closing or reactivating these orders and creates a compliance risk in our procurement records.

We kindly request that you confirm the following by end of business this week:

  1. Whether any goods or services were delivered against either purchase order.
  2. Whether any outstanding invoices or payment obligations remain.
  3. Your agreement to formally close both orders if no outstanding obligations exist.

Please contact our procurement team at your earliest convenience so we can resolve this matter and ensure our records accurately reflect the current state of our relationship.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request.

Best regards,
Procurement Team
Company Code 1710

Data source: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001) via SAP Business Accelerator Hub Sandbox. Analysis performed by

entry.title: SAP Customer Experience and the Autonomous Enterprise: Where AI Agents Meet Your Customers
entry.content:
SAP's Autonomous Enterprise isn't a CX story. It's an enterprise story, one where AI agents run business processes end to end, across finance, supply chain, procurement, HR, and CX, with minimal human intervention for routine work. CX is where that vision is most visible to your customers.

Understanding this distinction matters. If you think of the Autonomous Enterprise as an AI upgrade to your CRM, you'll underinvest in the infrastructure that makes it real. If you understand it as an enterprise-wide operating model powered by agentic AI, where Joule's collaborative agents work across functions simultaneously, you'll know where CX fits and how to build toward it.

This post covers both: what the Autonomous Enterprise actually is, and how SAP CX solutions and the Foundational Success Plan position you to realize that vision at the customer touchpoint.

What SAP's Autonomous Enterprise Actually Means

SAP defines the Autonomous Enterprise as a business that thinks, decides, and acts on its own, powered by AI agents working collaboratively across every function. The mechanism isn't AI that suggests the next step. It's AI that takes the next step.

At its core, it runs on three interlocking foundations:

Where Customer Experience Fits in the Autonomous Enterprise

Customer-facing processes are where the Autonomous Enterprise meets the people it serves. While AI agents are optimizing procurement cycles and closing financial periods in the background, your customers are experiencing the result: faster responses, more relevant offers, seamless service, and personalized commerce, without a team manually orchestrating any of it.

SAP's Customer Experience AI agents are the customer-facing execution layer of the Autonomous Enterprise. Several are already available, and more are on the roadmap:

These aren't add-ons. They're the agentic AI capabilities being built directly into SAP's Customer Experience portfolio as the customer-facing dimension of the Autonomous Enterprise.

The CX Solutions That Carry the Vision

The agentic AI layer runs on top of a mature, integrated CX portfolio. Some examples that agents need to act on:

  • SAP Sales Cloud Version 2: AI-guided selling, automated activity capture, and intelligent pipeline management — so your teams focus on the deals that matter most.

  • SAP Service Cloud Version 2: Intelligent case routing, automated resolution suggestions, and connected service experiences that reduce resolution time and improve customer satisfaction.

  • SAP Commerce Cloud: Composable commerce with AI-powered personalization, dynamic pricing, and real-time inventory visibility, built for scale and continuous improvement.

  • SAP Engagement Cloud: Customer engagement automation that delivers the right message at the right moment, powered by AI-driven segmentation and journey orchestration.

What connects them is SAP Business AI, embedded directly, so your teams don't have to switch tools or stitch together workarounds. The SAP Customer Experience AI Toolkit extends this further, providing a unified layer of AI capabilities across the entire CX portfolio.

Joule: The AI Agent Backbone for CX

SAP Joule isn't just an AI copilot that surfaces suggestions. In the context of the Autonomous Enterprise, Joule is the agent platform, the engine through which autonomous AI agents are deployed, orchestrated, and run across SAP solutions, including CX.

For CX specifically, Joule-powered agents handle tasks that previously required manual effort: drafting case summaries, generating knowledge articles, responding to customer questions in self-service flows, and briefing reps before customer conversations. As more agents are delivered, the scope expands, eventually covering the full customer lifecycle without a team manually driving each step.

What makes this different from earlier AI tools is the collaborative layer. Joule's agents don't work in isolation. A service agent and a supply chain agent can share context. A finance agent and a sales agent can coordinate. That's the enterprise-wide coherence that makes the Autonomous Enterprise real, not just faster individual tasks, but smarter cross-functional processes.

For customers pursuing autonomous CX, the Foundational Success Plan provides the operational foundation: validated best practices, proactive health monitoring, and access to expertise when you need it. You're not navigating the transformation alone.

Support Guides for Your CX Journey

The SAP Enterprise Support Value Map includes a set of curated guides built specifically for CX customers on the Autonomous Enterprise journey. They cover AI enablement, product foundations, and optimization practices, each validated, maintained, and accessible through SAP Enterprise Support.

AI Capabilities Across SAP Customer Experience Solutions: Discover how SAP Business AI capabilities work across SAP Commerce Cloud, SAP Sales Cloud, SAP Service Cloud, and SAP Engagement Cloud. Includes guidance on embedding AI into customer journeys and measuring business impact.

 

Foundational Resources for SAP Sales Cloud Version 2

Step into the next generation of selling. Essential guidance to implement, integrate, and optimize your modern sales platform.

Foundational Resources for SAP Service Cloud Version 2

Starting or transitioning to Service Cloud V2? Key insights to deploy confidently and deliver intelligent, connected service experiences.

Foundational Resources for SAP Commerce Cloud

New to Commerce Cloud or scaling further? Critical building blocks for stable deployments and continuous improvement.

Enhance Your SAP CX Experience with CQC Services

Learn how Continuous Quality Check (CQC) services from SAP Enterprise Support help you optimize your CX solutions post-go-live, covering performance, configuration, and adoption best practices.

All of these guides and more can be explored on our Navigation Hub, accessible through SAP Enterprise Support, part of the Foundational Success Plan included with your SAP cloud subscription.

Where to Start

The Autonomous Enterprise is an enterprise-wide transformation. For CX teams, the practical entry point is your existing SAP CX landscape, and the goal is to progressively activate the agentic AI capabilities that are being built into it.

If you're getting foundations right, start with the product-specific guides above. If you're ready to activate AI, the AI Capabilities Across SAP CX Solutions guide maps what's available and how to deploy it. If you want to understand the full picture, visit sap.com/products/autonomous-enterprise.html for SAP's official Autonomous Enterprise product page.

The Foundational Success Plan ensures your environment is ready to run new agentic AI capabilities as they arrive: the Digital Service Agent is available now, Automated Sales and Service Agents are delivered, and the Customer Insight Agent is on the roadmap for 2027. Clean core, continuous monitoring, and guided transformation aren't nice-to-haves in the Autonomous Enterprise. They're prerequisites.

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps  ​

​Beyond the Autonomous Enterprise, SAP Enterprise Support value maps on SAP Community provide guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next—all included in your SAP cloud subscription.​

​👉Visit us on SAP Community for more information and to get access. Don’t forget you can “Request a Call”  with one of our topic experts to connect you with the right enablement resources, fast. ​

entry.title: Re: LMS notifications
entry.content:
Hi Anna,

Based on the screenshot you provided, the section you’re referring to appears to be a Footer within the notification. This can typically be edited at the Organization level within your LMS settings, rather than at the individual notification level, so that would be a good place to start if you’re looking to modify that content.

Regarding the error/broken image visible in the notification, this is most likely caused by the image not being hosted on a publicly accessible server. LMS notifications require any embedded images to be reachable via a public URL. If the image is hosted on an internal or restricted server, it will fail to render correctly in the email notification. To resolve this, try re-uploading the image to a publicly accessible location, as per the KBA: 2157471 - Uploading Static Content and Generating a Public Link - SAP for Me, nd updating the URL in the notification template accordingly.

 

I hope this helps point you in the right direction!

 

Best Regards,

Duda

SAP Support

entry.title: Querying SAP SuccessFactors in Natural Language with Claude Desktop and the SF-MCP Server
entry.content:
How to connect an open-source Model Context Protocol server to Claude Desktop and interact with your SuccessFactors tenant using plain English - no OData knowledge required.

What is SF-MCP?

SF-MCP is an open-source Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that bridges Claude — Anthropic's AI assistant — to SAP SuccessFactors via its OData APIs. Instead of hand-crafting $filter expressions or memorising entity names, you just ask Claude in plain English and it translates your question into the right API call automatically.

 

Example interaction

You: "Who on the Engineering team has a work anniversary this month?"

Claude: Found 3 upcoming anniversaries — Jane Smith (5 years, March 12), 
Bob Johnson (2 years, March 18), Alice Chen (10 years, March 25).

The project lives at github.com/aiadiguru2025/sf-mcp and ships 43 purpose-built HR tools across 13 categories — covering everything from employee lookup and RBP security audits to hiring pipelines, time-off balances, and Integration Center job monitoring.

MCP is an open standard that lets AI assistants connect to external data sources in a secure, standardised way. Claude Desktop is the desktop client that makes it easy to wire up MCP servers locally without any coding.

Prerequisites

Before starting, make sure you have the following:

✅ Claude Desktop installed (download from claude.ai/download)
✅ Python 3.10 or newer — check with python3 --version
✅ Git installed
✅ A SAP SuccessFactors account with API access enabled
✅ Your company ID, data center code (e.g. DC55 for Frankfurt), and API user credentials

Note: Credentials are passed per-request and never stored in the config file. SF-MCP uses per-request authentication, so you will supply your user ID and password when you ask Claude each question.

Step-by-step setup

 

1. Install uv and clone the repository

SF-MCP uses uv, a fast Python package manager. Install it first, then clone the repo.

# Install uv (macOS / Linux)
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Install uv (Windows PowerShell)
powershell -c "irm https://astral.sh/uv/install.ps1 | iex"

# Clone the repo
git clone https://github.com/aiadiguru2025/sf-mcp.git
cd sf-mcp

# Install Python dependencies
uv sync

 
2. Find the path to the uv executable

Claude Desktop needs the absolute path to uv to launch the server. Run:

# macOS / Linux
which uv
# → /Users/yourname/.local/bin/uv

# Windows PowerShell
Get-Command uv | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Source
# → C:\Users\yourname\.local\bin\uv.exe

Copy this path — you will need it in the next step.

 
 
3. Edit the Claude Desktop configuration file

Open the config file for your operating system in any text editor:

# macOS
~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json

# Windows
%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json

Add the sf-mcp entry inside mcpServers. Replace the paths with the values you found in step 2:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "sf-mcp": {
      "command": "/Users/yourname/.local/bin/uv",
      "args": [
        "--directory",
        "/Users/yourname/projects/sf-mcp",
        "run",
        "main.py"
      ]
    }
  }
}
Tip (Windows): Use forward slashes or double backslashes in the JSON paths, e.g. C:\\Users\\yourname\\projects\\sf-mcp
 
4. Restart Claude Desktop and verify the connection

Fully quit Claude Desktop (not just close the window), then reopen it. After a few seconds you should see a hammer icon (🔨) appear in the message input area. Click it — you should see all 43 SF-MCP tools listed.

 
 
5. Run your first query

Now ask Claude a question in plain English. You will need to provide your SuccessFactors connection details the first time — Claude will prompt you for them or you can include them upfront:

 
 

The 43 tools at a glance

SF-MCP groups its tools into 13 categories. Here are some of the most useful ones for day-to-day HR and admin work:

Natural language query examples

Security considerations

Key security features you should be aware of:

🔐 Per-request credentials — your username and password are never stored in the config file or cached to disk. They are passed with each API call and masked in audit logs.
🛡️  Input validation — 10 regex-based validators protect against OData injection. Filter expressions, entity names, and $select/$orderby values are all sanitised.
🧩  XXE-safe XML parsing — the server uses defusedxml to prevent XML external entity attacks when processing OData metadata responses.
📋 Structured audit log — every tool call is logged with a request ID, timestamp, duration, and parameter summary (passwords redacted).

Troubleshooting tips

HTTP 401 — Authentication Error

Verify you are passing the user ID without the @instance suffix. For example use api_admin, not api_admin@ACME_CORP.

Server disconnects immediately in Claude Desktop

Server Disconnects in Claude Desktop

Run which uv again and confirm the path in your config is exactly right. Then test the server manually: uv run mcp dev main.py — this opens the MCP Inspector in your browser so you can call tools interactively.

Rate limit / 429 errors

Rate Limit / 429 Errors

The server automatically retries up to 3 times with a 5-second back-off. If you hit limits frequently, set SF_CACHE_TTL_DEFAULT in a .env file to cache read-heavy responses like picklists and metadata.

Final thoughts

I have created SF-MCP as one of the most complete open-source SuccessFactors API integrations available. Forty-three well-designed tools, solid input validation, production-grade caching and rate limiting, and a clean architecture that is easy to extend, all built on FastMCP, the leading Python MCP framework.

If you are an SF admin, HR tech consultant, or developer who regularly digs through OData APIs, this is worth setting up. The ability to ask Claude "compare User entity config between dev and prod" and get a structured diff instantly is a genuine time-saver.

Give it a ⭐ on GitHub and feel free to contribute - the project is MIT-licensed and I have laid out clear contribution guidelines in the README: github.com/aiadiguru2025/sf-mcp

Disclaimer: 

This project is provided for educational and experimental purposes only. It is offered as-is, with no warranties — express or implied. The author assumes no liability for any damages or issues arising from its use, including data loss, unintended API activity, or security vulnerabilities. Always validate behaviour in a non-production environment before wider use.

Before connecting SF-MCP repo to any SuccessFactors environment, please keep the following in mind:

  • Always start with a sandbox or preview instance - never point this at production as your first test
  • Grant your API user the minimum permissions needed and nothing more
  • Treat all tool outputs as informational — verify before taking any action based on them
  • Every query makes real, live OData API calls to your tenant. There is no simulation or dry-run mode

The author accepts no liability for any damages - direct or indirect - resulting from the use of this software. SF-MCP is an independent community project and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or supported by SAP SE or Anthropic.

entry.title: FS-CML: Output VAT (tax) line on interest not generated in FI document from FNM1 posting (S/4HANA 17
entry.content:
We have a legal requirement in Guatemala to post output VAT (IVA débito, 12%) on loan interest. When interest is posted via FNM1 (FS-CML), the FI document is created with only 2 lines (customer / interest income at gross), without the separate tax line in the G/L. The same posting done manually via FB01 correctly generates 3 lines (receivable / net income / VAT).

Environment: S/4HANA 1709, FS-CML, tax code A5 (output tax, KTOSL=MWS), tax determination maintained in V_AT210.

We have already tested, with debugging evidence, the following via BAdI FVD_LOAN_POST:

  • CHANGE_MWSKZ_IN_ACCIT: tax code A5 is correctly set on the income line, but it does not trigger any tax calculation (consistent with note 1009041).
  • CHANGE_TAX_DATA: populated C_TAB_ACCTX (ACCBSET) and C_TAB_ACCTAX (tax distribution, MWSK1/DMBT1), individually and combined, per note 1476622. The tables are correctly passed to AC_DOCUMENT_CREATE (verified in LTRDBF02), but RWIN does not generate the tax G/L line — document stays at 2 lines.
  • CHANGE_ACC_DATA adding the VAT line to C_TAB_ACCIT: discarded by the standard check CHECK l_lines_old = l_lines_new in form CHECK_DOCUMENT_IN_FI (LTRDBF02), which rejects any line-count change.
  • BAdI AC_DOCUMENT (CHANGE_INITIAL): structure ACC_DOCUMENT_SUBST only substitutes fields, cannot add lines.

KBA 2492835 confirms CML does not support FI-style automatic tax calculation, and only points to BAdI workarounds.

Question: Is there a supported way to have the tax line on interest generated automatically in the FI document from a CML posting? Or is CREATE_OWN_POSTING (building the full document ourselves via BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST) the only viable path? If so, how does the own-posting document reconnect to the loan flow so that reversal (FNM1S) works correctly and the subledger link is preserved?

entry.title: Storage location determination for delivery
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to configure stock determination for sales delivery. As part of it, we have used the standard stock determination group 001 and assigned it to the material and also the stock determination rule 001 to movement type 601 and also for the item category TAN.

 But even after this, the system is not automatically determining the storage location in neither in the outbound document or while posting for goods issue. 

our requirement is that we have already enabled batch determination. but during batch determination, it is fetching the batches from all storage locations under the plant. we want to restrict this to certain list of storage locations. 

we also understand that there is a standard storage location determination for SD but that gives us the option only to assign one storage location for a shipping point+plant+storage condition combination. we actually have a list of storage locations from which batch determination is allowed.

please guide on what are the possible solutions that we are missing out on. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Storage location determination for delivery
entry.content:
Dear customer,

We reviewed the scenario described regarding stock determination, storage location determination, and batch determination during outbound delivery processing.

According to the behavior described, batches from multiple storage locations within the plant are currently being considered during batch determination.

Regarding the configuration already maintained:

Stock Determination Group 0001
Stock Determination Rule Y 0001
Assignment to movement type 601
Assignment to item category TAN

This configuration alone did not result in automatic storage location determination in the outbound delivery for the scenario described.

In standard SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SD Storage Location Determination can be configured based on combinations such as Shipping Point, Plant, and Storage Conditions.

For the requirement described, we could not identify a standard configuration option to maintain multiple allowed storage locations specifically for SD batch determination processing.

Therefore, for business requirements where batch determination should only consider a predefined list of storage locations, it may be necessary to evaluate extensibility options available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

One relevant enhancement option available for delivery processing is the following BAdI:

  • LE_SHP_STORAGE_LOC_DETERMINE

Official SAP documentation:

  • Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for Delivery Management
  • Delivery Management

Best regards,
Letícia

entry.title: Re: Linking between Installment Invoice and its payment
entry.content:
Good day @LALIT_RAH1 

Thank you for your question.

For your requirement, the link between an installment and the payment is not held through a special installment-payment relationship field. It is created through open item clearing. Note that an item or group of items is considered cleared when an offset value is posted so that the resulting balance is zero.

In the custom CDS report, start from the customer receivable line items that you create from the billing document. Treat each installment as a separate customer line item with its own due date and amount. Then use the clearing information, especially Clearing Journal Entry, Clearing Fiscal Year and Clearing Date, to identify which payment or clearing document settled that installment.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/64d8ff166ec447faa46061706c83f678.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/f585d657f8a28a3de10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

If one incoming payment clears multiple instalments, the same clearing document can appear against multiple installment lines. If partial or residual clearing is used, include follow-on item logic in the report. See KBA 3558776 for more details.

I suggest the following:

  • Validate the relationship first in Manage Customer Line Items.                                                                            
  • Add the columns for Clearing Journal Entry, Clearing Date, Net Due Date, Payment Terms, and Follow-On Doc.Type.
  • Then reproduce the same logic in your custom CDS by linking the installment receivable item to the payment or clearing document through the clearing fields.

To summarise:

  1. As a best practice, build the report from the FI cleared item logic, not from the billing document alone. The clean reporting logic is:
  2. Billing document → accounting receivable installment line item → clearing journal entry → incoming payment journal entry.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Payment methods are not inherited from selected open...
entry.content:
Good day @yukitsumura 

Thank you for your question.

The answers to your main question are as follows:

There is no standard configuration, app setting, substitution, or released enhanced option in Clear Incoming Payments to automatically inherit Payment Method, Baseline Date for Due Date Calculation, and Cash Discount Days 1 from the selected original open item into the new clearing/payment document.

For partial incoming payments, the system does not copy the baseline date or payment terms from the original invoice document to the partial payment document, and this behaviour is standard. Since Cash Discount Days 1 is part of the payment terms logic, do not expect it to be inherited into the partial payment document either.

The standard workaround is for you to use Residual Amount instead of partial payment where the business process allows it. For residual payment, the payment terms and baseline date are copied from the original invoice document. See KBA 3080455 for more details.

Key user extensibility does not provide a solution for copying these selected open item fields during clearing. Custom fields for Post Incoming/Outgoing Payments and Clear Incoming/Outgoing Payments are not supported in the AP/AR Open Items and Proposed Items tabs. See KBA 3477046 for more insights.

As a next step, test the behaviour in Manage Customer Line Items

 and Clear Incoming Payments  by comparing the original invoice fields with the generated payment or clearing document. 

 

If the requirement is reporting, use the clearing relationship to display these fields from the original invoice item instead of trying to copy them to the payment document. If the requirement is mandatory field inheritance during clearing, this should be submitted as an improvement request.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2605
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2605

On May 16, the new Release 2605 was delivered. This blog post lists all delivered features and provides an overview. Highlights of the release include new ABAP Language features, the Manage News app for MyHome, the Review Booklets for embedded analytics, and the new Manage SAP System Upgrade app.

The list of all new features including more information can be found on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP environment page. The new AI features are listed separately on the What's New for ABAP Generative AI in ABAP Cloud. The first delivery of ADT for VS Code is independent from the ABAP Backend release and will be rolled-out separately. 

ABAP Language

  • Support of boolean expressions
  • Recursive references as enhancement of the ABAP Type System 
  • ABAP statement DELETE DUPLICATES without sorting

ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  • Signature comparison during CDS Migration from DDIC-based views to CDS view entities (see Signature Comparison and Migrating CDS DDIC-based Views to CDS View Entities)
  • Using bracket highlighting for ABAP CDS objects
  • WYSIWYG editor support for Review Booklet pages with two data providers (details)

ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP)

  • Support of RAP Feature Control attributes readonly:create and mandatory:update (details)
  • Support of CDS Table Entities as draft persistence in RAP (details)

Analytics

  • Flexible Content Types for Review Booklets with Multiple Data Providers (details)
  • Enhanced App State Handling for Review Booklets (details)

Reuse Services

  • XCO Generation API for CDS Table Entities (details)
  • Maintain Translations app for the translation of email templates (details)

Business Configuration

  • You can now select Tree Table as table type in the SMBC object (details)

ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC)

  • Integration of ATC system in SAP BTP with ChaRM (details)
  • Improved ATC Check Results and Quick Fixes for SAP S/4HANA Custom Code Analysis 
  • New ATC Check Variant for Clean Core ABAP_CLEAN_CORE_READINESS to perform Clean Core analysis on SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA systems

Custom Code Analysis

  • Update accessible resources in Cloud Connector (see SAP Note 2861842 and SAP Note 3548772)
  • Manage Number of ATC Processes via Elastic Scaling (details)

SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • New app Manage News to author and manage news content that appears on the My Home page (details)
  • Reset Single Parameters in the Manage Launchpad Settings App (details)

Landscape Portal

  • Deploy Product: Install multiple add-ons in the same system (details)
  • New app Manage SAP System Upgrade (details)

Infrastructure

  • Transparent start time of the technical downtime for the next quartely upgrade (details)
  • Reduced minimum ABAP Runtime size with 0.5 ACU (details)
  • New Multi-Availability Zone Option with SAP HANA Cloud database configuration in a high-availability setup with a primary and a secondary instance in different availability zones (details)
  • Two new regions on Microsoft Azure: Toronto / Canada and Frankfurt / Germany (details)

entry.title: Re: Inspection Type 02 Goods Issue inspections 06 Inspect...
entry.content:
Hello @sakar_korde94c0f8 

Thank you for your question.

You cannot freely activate classic inspection types 02 and 06 if they are not delivered in the Public Cloud. The delivered inspection type variants in cloud are restricted and cannot simply be changed or replaced like in on-premise system. See KBA 2765771 for more insights.

For goods issue related quality checks, use the delivered Quality Management in Sales 1MP process. This process supports quality inspection during the sales process and after the inspection is accepted, goods issue is posted and the material is delivered to the customer.

For customer returns, use the delivered Customer Returns BKP process with product inspection instead of trying to activate inspection type 06. Customer Returns supports product inspection and logistical follow-up activities. The inspection result can be entered using Enter Inspection Results From Warehouse, where inspection code, comments, inspector and follow-up activity are maintained.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/a3d12e631d814c7cac20ef34f1eaab6d.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/530018c2f7834bf694660554efbb9dd7.html?locale=en-US

I suggest as a next step for you to test the outbound quality check with a sales order, outbound delivery, inspection result recording and goods issue. For returns, test with Manage Customer Returns,  

post the return receipt, enter inspection results and confirm the follow-up activity. If the business specifically requires the classic inspection type numbers 02 and 06, you should treat this as a fit gap and raise an incident or feature request.

 

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Latest Home Page Onboarding External User
entry.content:
Hi Emmanuel,

Quick Actions are displayed based on user permissions and can be globally enabled or disabled via the Enabled switch under Manage Home Page > Quick Actions. While admins have the ability to hide a Quick Action for all users, there is currently no option to restrict visibility to a specific group.

Regarding the "Manage My Data" behavior you mentioned, this is system behavior as outlined in KBA 3399454 - "Manage my Data" Quick Action card does not display content. There is an active Customer Influence idea open for this scenario, and we encourage you to check it out and add your vote to help push it forward: Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence.

entry.title: Administrator Enablement: Building the Safety Net That Sustains SuccessFactors Value
entry.content:
Why Administrator Enablement Is a Strategic Lever—Not an Operational Detail

SuccessFactors is a living platform. Semesterly releases, evolving business needs, and organizational change require constant system stewardship.

Without properly enabled administrators:

  • Configurations drift from original business intent
  • Processes become inefficient or inconsistent
  • Innovation from releases is underutilized
  • Dependency on external support increases
  • Value realization stalls

Administrator enablement is therefore not just about knowledge—it is about sustaining transformation outcomes.

A More Mature Approach: From Training to a Competence Model

A key insight from recent expert discussions is the need to move beyond traditional enablement into a Customer Competence Center model, supported by a maturity framework and accreditation-driven capability building.

This introduces a more structured concept:

Administrator enablement as a capability system—not a training event.

At the core of this approach is ensuring that:

  • Organizations have sufficient expert coverage across all SuccessFactors modules
  • Each expert is backed by formal accreditation aligned to their domain
  • Capabilities are distributed to support the end-to-end employee lifecycle

This creates a true operational safety net, ensuring that no critical area of the solution is unmanaged or under-skilled.

What This Looks Like in Practice

Leading organizations are shifting toward a Competence Center of Expertise (CoE) model, where internal teams act as the custodians of the platform.

In practice, this means:

  • Defining domain ownership (e.g., Recruiting, Onboarding, Employee Central, Performance, Compensation)
  • Expertise: Ensuring at least one accredited expert per domain, with depth in both process and configuration
  • Building redundancy and collaboration across roles to avoid single points of failure
  • Establishing the CoE as the central authority for governance, changes, and optimization

For example, in organizations already live with modules such as Recruiting, Onboarding, and Employee Central, a minimum baseline would include:

  • Certified expertise in Candidate Experience, Recruiter Experience, and Onboarding
  • Dedicated ownership of core HR (Employee Central)
  • Capability to expand into People Analytics and Talent Intelligence

This is not about over-resourcing—it is about ensuring coverage and continuity.

From Safety Net to Self-Sustaining System

When done correctly, this model creates what can be described as a “virtuous cycle”:

  • Internal expertise increases
  • Dependency on external support decreases
  • Issues are resolved faster and more effectively
  • Adoption of new capabilities improves
  • Strategic conversations replace operational escalations

Organizations that adopt this approach consistently show:

  • Greater platform stability
  • Stronger stakeholder confidence
  • More strategic engagement with SAP
  • Higher long-term ROI from SuccessFactors

In short, the system becomes self-sustaining rather than reactive.

Organizations that recognize this move from maintaining systems to sustaining business value at scale.

entry.title: Re: SQLMD: Cross Node Joins Analysis
entry.content:
Found the answer here:

M_CS_COLUMNS has details of tables, columns and partitions and the following example may be helpful to check the NUMA locations where tables are distributed across NUMA nodes. In this case CPU load is distributed across NUMA nodes by NUMA-local processing, but memory consumption is also better distributed to the memory attached to each NUMA node.

select table_name, column_name, part_id, string_agg(numa_node_index, ',') from m_cs_columns 
group by table_name, column_name, part_id 
order by table_name, column_name, part_id;
